title: ceratozamia miqueliana
intro: Ceratozamia miqueliana is a species of cycad, a type of ancient, non-vascular plant that belongs to the family Zamiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating pain, and is also used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Ceratozamia miqueliana are often dried and used to make tea or infusions, which are consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
